repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Add a simple AGENTS.md rule for the AI-Assisted trailer in commit
messages and a workflow that adds the AI assisted label when PR commits
include that trailer.

comment_data.body:
use of fundamentally insecure workflow trigger

[Show more details](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/security/code-scanning/74)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+name: "labeler: AI assisted"
+on:
+  pull_request_target:
+    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]

comment_data.body: This is an ultra nit but most Git trailers don't use Title Case, but only upper case the first word, e.g. `Co-authored-by` or `Signed-off-by`. In which case we should make this `AI-assisted`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+# AGENTS.md
+
+## AI Disclosure
+
+If AI was used in any way for a commit, add an `AI-Assisted: <tool name>` trailer to the commit message.

comment_data.body: (Misclicked; as a card-carrying pedant I wanted to approve of this nit.)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+# AGENTS.md
+
+## AI Disclosure
+
+If AI was used in any way for a commit, add an `AI-Assisted: <tool name>` trailer to the commit message.

comment_data.body: The check is case-insensitive. I'll change this to `AI-assisted`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+# AGENTS.md
+
+## AI Disclosure
+
+If AI was used in any way for a commit, add an `AI-Assisted: <tool name>` trailer to the commit message.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+# AGENTS.md
+
+## AI Disclosure
+
+If AI was used in any way for a commit, add an `AI-Assisted: <tool name>` trailer to the commit message.

comment_regular_data.body: The only reason is that the other labeler workflow only triggers on PR opened. I can look at consolidating them if you want.
not a blocker, but these jobs do have a security cost so minimizing them has value.
Does this also trigger for "Co-authored by: Claude" commit messages?

> Does this also trigger for "Co-authored by: Claude" commit messages?

Does now.
I've also seen "Copilot", "OpenCode", "Codex", and "Amp" in the wild. Not sure if it makes sense to track all possible agents' bylines, but I guess the cost is not high?
I've tried to add a few very broad pattern. Added: OpenCode and Amp.

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

labels.description: automation for build, test, and release
labels.name: ci

labels.description: May contain trace amounts of GMO
labels.name: AI assisted 🤖

title: ci: label disclosed AI-assisted PRs
